intro: Deyeuxia stenophylla, also known as Chinese wild grass or Tibetan grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China, Tibet, and Nepal. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and rheumatism, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia stenophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Deyeuxia stenophylla is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
